title: Mildly Spicy Tunisian Chicken Tagine
description: A light and flavorful Tunisian dish featuring chicken, peanut cookies, and a hint of mustard, perfect for a date night, and diabetic-friendly due to its balanced ingredients and cooking method in a wood-fired oven, wrapped in a corn husk vessel.
instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Wrap the corn husks around a heat-proof vessel to create a unique cooking vessel.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, mustard, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chicken to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Add the crushed peanut cookies, chopped dates, and chopped parsley to the bowl and toss to combine.
Place the vessel wrapped in corn husks in the wood-fired oven and bake for 30 minutes.
Reduce the oven temperature to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and continue baking for an additional 20 minutes.
Remove the vessel from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Serve the chicken tagine hot, garnished with additional chopped parsley if desired.
